metadata.title:
By OFFERING A COMPROMISE IN
TIE MATTER OF OPENING
STREETS IN BROWNIES add
TON.

pages.raw_text:
c. M. Levey, assistant to the presi-
dent of the Northern Pacific: Division
Superintendent A. Beamer, Attorney
Bum, counsel for the Northern PACT
flc; Commissioners Root, Handley and
Weymouth and Councilmen Gray and
Koontz assembled this morning at
1890 o'clock and together went to
Brownies addition to make a close
scrutiny and study of the railway
crossings in that section of the city.


Some time ago the property hold
ers whose ground lies near these
crossings, and the city, took the mat
ter of the opening of the streets rum
ming east and west in that section
to the courts. looking for a decision
from 1t opening Third, Fourth and
Fifth avenues to public traffic. There
has as yet been no decision.


On their arrival on the spot the
city representatives were given q
thorough surprise by Mr.. Levey. He
now offers a proposition which on the
surface would look as If the Northern
Pacific people expect decision of the
courts against them and In favor of
the city. The proposition they Of
fered is to close Third. Fourth and
Fifth avenues, but to open the small
streets running north and south. The
city representatives listened to this
scheme and gave II considerable dis
cussion, but from Individual ex-
pressions the scheme Will not be con-
